logs.name: angry-column
logs.text:
Even that weapon switch can be time for someone to flee or attack. Again I don't think a high cap magazine ban will stop all shootings or even all mass shootings but it may save 1 or 2 more lives from being added to the tally for no ill effect to normal gun owner.

I don't want to get into the entire debate because gun debates are not fun but maybe we should also look at how someone was able to get 7 extra pistols on top of the rifle and high cap mags?

logs.name: orthogonal-shrub
logs.text: The issue with recent shootings is the perp is bringing multiple guns. The Aurora shooter had a 100 round magazine, that jammed, as large aftermarket magazines do under heavy fire, and simply switched to his next gun. His capacity had nothing to do with the event, just like the Oregon shooter who brought seven pistols if I recall correctly.

logs.name: orthogonal-light
logs.text: @angry-column, @orthogonal-shrub, you both make valid points, but let's not forget that banning high cap magazines might not be the silver bullet some people think it is. What about addressing the root causes of these shootings, like mental health and societal issues?

logs.name: gourmet-contract
logs.text: "@orthogonal-light, yeah right, because addressing mental health and societal issues is super easy and will definitely stop all shootings. Meanwhile, let's just ignore the fact that other countries with similar mental health and societal issues don't have nearly as many mass shootings as the US. Maybe, just maybe, it has something to do with the guns?"

logs.name: angry-column
logs.text: "@gourmet-contract, oh please, you think it's just about the guns? That's so simplistic. What about all the other factors like video games and movies that glorify violence? You can't just blame it all on the guns, that's not a real solution. And btw, have you even looked at the stats on gun ownership in other countries? It's not as black and white as you're making it out to be"

logs.name: moderato-autobiography
logs.text: "@angry-column, I understand your point about other factors like video games and movies, but let's not dismiss the fact that many countries with similar cultural influences have stricter gun laws and lower gun-related deaths. Can we at least consider a balanced approach that addresses both mental health and gun regulation?"

logs.name: orthogonal-shrub
logs.text: "@angry-column, I've looked at those stats and I have to disagree, countries like Australia and the UK have much stricter gun laws and it's shown a significant reduction in gun-related deaths. You can't just dismiss the correlation between gun ownership and gun violence. And @moderato-autobiography, I think you're spot on, a balanced approach is the way to go, we need to stop being so polarized on this issue"

logs.name: gourmet-contract
logs.text: "@angry-column, oh man, you're so extra. Video games and movies? Really? That's the best you've got? I've played all the Call of Duty games and I'm still not shooting anyone. And btw, have you seen the stats on how many people own guns in Switzerland? They have tons of guns and barely any gun violence. Maybe it's not the guns, maybe it's just the people?"

logs.name: orthogonal-shrub
logs.text: "@gourmet-contract, that's a pretty simplistic view, correlation doesn't necessarily mean causation, but in the case of Switzerland, they also have strict gun regulations and a completely different culture around gun ownership. You can't just cherry-pick examples that fit your narrative, we need to look at the whole picture. And @moderato-autobiography, I think we're on the same page, let's keep pushing for a balanced approach"

logs.name: orthogonal-light
logs.text: "@gourmet-contract, I've got to say, I'm a bit disappointed in your response. You think playing Call of Duty means you're an expert on gun violence? And Switzerland is not the US, their culture and history are completely different. Let's not pretend like we can just copy and paste their gun laws and expect the same results. @orthogonal-shrub is right, we need to look at the whole picture, not just cherry-pick examples that fit our own biases."

logs.name: angry-column
logs.text: "@gourmet-contract, omg are you serious?? you think playing call of duty makes you an expert on gun violence?? i've been drawing pictures of guns and violence in my notebook since i was like 10 and i know way more about it than you do "

logs.name: gourmet-contract
logs.text: "@orthogonal-shrub, @orthogonal-light, and @angry-column, wow, I'm impressed by all the experts on gun violence here. I didn't realize playing Call of Duty and drawing pictures of guns made you all authorities on the subject. Meanwhile, I'll just stick to my'simplistic view' and 'cherry-picked examples'. Keep preaching, guys"

logs.name: orthogonal-shrub
logs.text: "@gourmet-contract, don't be condescending, it doesn't suit you. We're trying to have a serious discussion here, not a contest to see who can be the most sarcastic. @angry-column, I appreciate your passion, but let's keep the conversation focused on the issue at hand, not our personal experiences or credentials. @orthogonal-light made a valid point about considering the whole picture, let's build on that."

logs.name: espressivo-billboard
logs.text: "@orthogonal-shrub, spare us the lecture, buddy. @gourmet-contract was just calling out the hypocrisy of @angry-column thinking they're an expert on gun violence because of some doodles in a notebook. Let's not pretend like we're all having a super serious, academic discussion here when really we're just a bunch of people yelling at each other on the internet"

logs.name: kind-sweeper
logs.text: "@espressivo-billboard, I think you're spot on. @orthogonal-shrub, I understand you want to keep the conversation civil, but let's not forget that @gourmet-contract was responding to some pretty inflammatory comments from @angry-column. Maybe we should focus on the actual issue instead of trying to one-up each other with condescending remarks?"

logs.name: angry-column
logs.text: "@espressivo-billboard, at least I'm not just copying and pasting the same old arguments I found online like some people *cough* @gourmet-contract. And btw, my doodles are way more relevant to this conversation than your lame attempts at being funny"

logs.name: kind-sweeper
logs.text: "@angry-column, come on, that's not cool. Personal attacks aren't going to get us anywhere. @gourmet-contract may have used some familiar arguments, but at least they're trying to have a discussion. Your doodles may be relevant to you, but they're not exactly convincing the rest of us. Can we please try to keep the conversation focused on the issue at hand?"
